summary: Frontend-only regression executed on public URL for expanded issuer detail sheet. All requested feature flows passed: insight badges + analyst angle, clickable tabs (Overview/Financials/Dividend/Valuation), financial tables, dividend history with payout/yield chart, valuation summary with ratio trend chart, sector benchmark comparison, metric tone badges, and detail-sheet stability across multiple issuers.

ui_bugs.issue: Benchmark card label typo uses 'Emitten' instead of 'Emiten'.

integration_issues.flow: Open detail sheet and switch tabs containing Recharts
integration_issues.issue: Console repeatedly logs Recharts warning: width(-1)/height(-1) should be greater than 0 during sheet/tab transitions.

action_items: Investigate/reduce Recharts container sizing warnings when charts render inside sheet/tabs.
Optionally add explicit container sizing guards or delayed chart mount for hidden tab content.
critical_code_review_comments: No blocking frontend functional defects found in the requested scope.
Data-testid coverage for tested detail-sheet interactions is good and automation-friendly.
Runtime chart sizing warnings indicate non-fatal rendering lifecycle issue worth cleanup before scale-up.
seed_data_creation: None. Fundamental detail remains STATIC SEEDED FRONTEND DATASET (NOT API-MOCKED ENDPOINTS).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL tested: https://fundamental-checker.preview.emergentagent.com. Verified detail sheet flows for BBRI + multi-emiten stability (ADRO, TLKM, BBRI). One prior failure was script-level assumption (non-visible row), corrected by using visible rows and sheet scroll handling.
rca of the issue: Recharts warnings likely occur because ResponsiveContainer initializes while parent tab/sheet content is not fully measurable (hidden or transitioning), producing temporary negative/invalid dimensions. Reproduction: open detail sheet, switch tabs with charts, observe console warnings. Mitigation: render charts only when tab active and container has non-zero width/height, or trigger chart mount after a small layout-settle delay.
